summary: electron-orbit exfiltrates installer environment variables to Azure Blob endpoint via hand-rolled TLS client
details: electron-orbit@1.0.29 is a hostile package impersonating the archived orbitdb-archive/orbit-electron project. On npm install, the postinstall hook (install.js, referenced from package.json scripts.postinstall) iterates process.env, filters out only keys containing 'path', JSON-serializes the remaining environment (including CI secrets, tokens, and cloud credentials), and writes the dump to os.tmpdir()/electron_orbit_install_marker.txt. On module load (require/import of dist/index.js), setupAutoRenderOnImport() reads the staged marker file, XORs its contents with the key 'electron-orbit', hex-encodes them, and appends them as a query string to a URL assembled from XOR-decoded fragments resolving to electronorbit.blob.core.windows.net/icons/orbit.png. The request is delivered by an in-package, from-scratch TLS 1.3 implementation built on node:net (raw ClientHello, X25519 key share, HKDF-Expand-Label, AES-128-GCM record layer, ALPN 'http/1.1'), bypassing Node's tls module and performing no server certificate validation, which evades corporate TLS inspection and endpoint DLP. Activation of the exfil host is gated by a SHA-256 check on process.env.BuildType against a hardcoded hash prefix, so the package appears inert outside the attacker's targeted build environments. String constants ('process', 'env', 'node:net', 'connect', HTTP request line, Host header, User-Agent 'aetherget/...', ALPN, and the destination hostname) are stored as XOR-encoded byte arrays decoded by an inline __s/l helper to defeat plaintext scanning.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
